title: Doctrine text stops misinforming Codex sessions, with the counts kept straight
action: Per D1/D2/D21/D22/D24/D25. FOUR COUNTS EXIST AND THEY ARE ALL TRUE OF DIFFERENT THINGS — measure before you touch any of them: `ls hooks/bee-*.mjs` = 9 hook SCRIPTS; `Object.keys(require('./.codex/hooks.json').hooks).length` = 8 Codex LIFECYCLE EVENTS; the same expression on hooks/claude-hooks.json = 7 Claude lifecycle events; `Object.keys(require('./.bee/config.json').hooks).length` = 6 CONFIG TOGGLES, which test_onboard_bee.mjs:457 asserts green. A prose claim of 'six' is CORRECT when it means the toggles (docs/06-runtime-integration.md:120 'all six hooks default-on, each toggleable' is true — leave it). Fix only claims where the number is wrong for the quantity named. STEP 1 — rewrite the guardrails sentence at skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md:80 to state exactly three things: both runtimes ship hooks rendered from one shared catalog (.codex/hooks.json, 8 lifecycle events, onboarding-tracked at .bee/onboarding.json:51); the enforcement floor is the shared helpers, identical on both; and Codex hook EXECUTION is unverified, so self-honoring remains the fallback on any runtime whose hook execution is unconfirmed. STEP 2 — correct the stale corners: docs/02-architecture.md:219, :221, :223; README.md:312, :444 ('7 lifecycle events' -> 8), :479; INSTALL.md:63; docs/01-distillation.md:36 and :111 ('exactly 6 hooks wrapping shared lib/ modules'); docs/05-roadmap.md:69; docs/decisions/0002-scribing-skill.md:12 ('The 6-hook cap is unchanged'); docs/06-runtime-integration.md:40 and :5-6 (the 'Claude Code gets a hook-driven skeleton / Codex gets the helper-enforced skeleton' framing that survives the sentence fix verbatim); docs/07-contracts.md:104 and :216. STEP 3 — regen chain, in this order (D24): `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, then `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`, then `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`. The manifest hashes skills/** and hooks/** (release_manifest.mjs:131,133), so editing the template moves release_manifest --check; onboard --apply does NOT render the two plugin trees, only render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs does, so rendering must precede the manifest write or stale trees get frozen as authoritative. STEP 4 — scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs hard-fails at 20480 bytes; AGENTS.md measured 19181 before this change. If the rewrite pushes past it, tighten the new wording.

truths: The retired sentence appears in zero live copies: the template, all four projections, and the merged root AGENTS.md.
The replacement preserves the unverified-execution caveat — no claim of proven Codex hook parity.
Every corrected count matches its own quantity, and the TRUE six-toggle claims are left intact.
AGENTS.md stays under 20480 bytes, achieved by tightening wording and never by raising the threshold.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: Rewritten doctrine sentence, corrected corners, regenerated projections and plugin trees, refreshed manifest and ledger
prohibitions: Never edit AGENTS.md's bee block by hand as the primary fix — it is merged from the template.
Never raise HARD_FAIL_BYTES or WARN_BYTES in scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs.
Never 'fix' docs/06-runtime-integration.md:120's six-toggle claim — it is true.
Do not touch docs/history/** — archived reports are not live copies.

verify: node scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/run_verify.mjs

title: A doctrine gate that derives its own scan set, knows four counts apart, and bites
action: Per D22/D25. Author scripts/test_doctrine_parity.mjs (auto-discovered under scripts/, run_verify.mjs:40-45). (1) DERIVE THE SCAN SET, do not assume it: enumerate tracked markdown via `git ls-files '*.md'` and exclude docs/history/** (archive) — the scope is itself a derived quantity, and the exclusion list is the only hand-written part, stated as such. (2) FILE SET for the retired sentence: glob **/AGENTS.block.md PLUS the merged root AGENTS.md explicitly — the glob does NOT match the root file, and the root file is the one every session loads. Assert zero occurrences. (3) FOUR DERIVED COUNTS, each compared only against prose that names ITS quantity: hook scripts from a glob of hooks/bee-*.mjs (9); Codex lifecycle events from Object.keys(json.hooks).length of .codex/hooks.json (8) — NOT Object.keys(json), which is ['hooks'], length 1; Claude lifecycle events the same way from hooks/claude-hooks.json (7); config toggles from Object.keys(config.hooks).length of .bee/config.json (6). A claim of 'six hooks' meaning toggles must PASS; a claim of 'six hooks' meaning scripts must FAIL. Encode that distinction explicitly — if the two cannot be told apart from the surrounding prose, say so in the failure message rather than guessing. (4) --selftest, following scripts/release_manifest.mjs:239-292 and scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs:113-129: run the checker against fixture roots — one with the sentence re-introduced into a root-AGENTS.md-shaped file, one whose prose count disagrees with its derived count — and assert both are reported. This requires the checker be factored to take a root/file-set parameter; do that.

truths: The gate fails when the retired sentence is re-introduced into the merged root AGENTS.md specifically, not only into a projection.
The gate fails when a prose count disagrees with the quantity it names, and PASSES on a true six-toggle claim.
Both failure modes are demonstrated by --selftest with its output recorded, not asserted.
The scan set is derived from git ls-files; no projection path list and no expected count is hand-maintained.
artifacts: scripts/test_doctrine_parity.mjs with a --selftest exhibiting both bites
prohibitions: Never hand-maintain the file list or the expected counts — derive both.
Never let the AGENTS.block.md glob stand in for the root AGENTS.md.
Never collapse the four counts into one number.

title: One shared compaction module: log, counts, sweep, warning, anchor predicate
action: Per D3/D4/D5/D9/D10/D12/D13/D17/D24. Create skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/compaction.mjs — the one module both hooks and verbs call. Exports: (1) appendCompactionRecord(root,{event,sessionId}) writing .bee/logs/compaction.jsonl via fsutil appendJsonl (it calls ensureDir; a bare fs.appendFileSync would throw ENOENT), inside a LOCAL try/catch so a write failure never changes any caller's return value or exit code (precedent hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs:106-128). Record per D5: {ts,event,session,lane,feature,phase,cell,compact_index,cell_compact_count,anchor_present}, with lane/feature/phase resolved via resolvePipeline(root,{sessionId}) — WITH the id, as inject.mjs:301-305 and :477-479 both do. ONLY precompact records are counted and only a precompact counts itself: precompact = prior + 1; resume = the plain prior count, never +1 again. (2) readCompactionCounts(root,{sessionId,cell}). (3) survivalWarning(count) -> the D9 string when >= 2, else null. (4) anchorMissing(root,{sessionId}) per D10: phase non-terminal (terminal = idle | compounding-complete, state.mjs:1655) AND (cell claimed by this session OR approved_gates.execution) AND readIntent null; returns the exact command to run. (5) compactCheck(root,{sessionId}) per D12, read-only, surfacing LANE_INVALID/LANE_MISSING/LANE_CORRUPT rather than swallowing them, and reporting session-less reservation rows (reservations.mjs:92-98) as 'unbound', never a mismatch. THEN the D24 regen chain in this same cell: mirror to .bee/bin/lib/, `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`, `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`. A new lib file moves BOTH release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check; the plugin trees must be rendered before the manifest write or the stale trees are frozen as authoritative. Author scripts/test_compaction_module.mjs: two compaction cycles asserting D9 fires on the SECOND not the first; a read-only .bee/logs/ case asserting the caller's exit code is unchanged; a lane-bound session asserting the record carries the lane's feature/phase; a corrupt lane asserting the typed code surfaces; and idempotence proven by hashing the .bee/ tree before and after two compactCheck runs.

truths: A resume record never re-increments the counts — one compaction reads as one.
compactCheck leaves the .bee/ tree hash-identical across two consecutive runs; the hash is the proof, not the stdout.
A corrupt lane record surfaces its typed LANE_* code.
A failed log append leaves the caller's return value and exit code unchanged.
release_manifest --check AND ledger_parity --check are both green at cap time.
artifacts: compaction.mjs + mirror, scripts/test_compaction_module.mjs, regenerated plugin trees, manifest and ledger
prohibitions: Never edit .bee/bin/lib/ as the source (D17).
The sweep never repairs, releases, or blocks.
No log rotation or size cap.
Never run release_manifest --write before rendering the plugin trees.

verify: node scripts/test_compaction_module.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

title: Two verbs: compact-log and compact-check
action: Per D3/D17/D24. Register TWO verbs only — `state compact-log --event <precompact|resume> --session-id <id> [--json]` and `state compact-check --session-id <id> [--json]` — following state.handoff.write (command-registry.mjs:877-905 entry shape; handler in skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs; one line in the name-to-handler map at bee.mjs:4992-4994). `state compact-capsule` is deliberately NOT registered here: test_bee_cli.mjs:2558 asserts every registry entry had its example executed, so a verb whose builder does not exist yet turns the repo red. It lands in cz-5 with its builder. THREE CHANGE SITES BEYOND THE REGISTRY, all of which a naive implementation misses: (a) skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs — `executedNames` grows only via explicit runExample/assertExampleOk calls (:148), so each new entry needs its own call or the coverage check at :2558-2563 fails; (b) skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs:4830, the `state` group's `Unknown command … Use: …` list, which the DA5 bijection check at test_bee_cli.mjs:276-303 parses in BOTH directions — the new verbs must appear there; (c) FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS (bee.mjs:5067) already contains `json`, and these verbs add no other bare boolean, so nothing is needed there — stated so you do not go looking. compact-check's JSON output includes an anchor_missing check carrying the same command D10's nudge names, which is how the nudge stays reachable without a hook (D3). Then the D24 regen chain: mirror both edited runtime files to .bee/bin/, render the plugin trees, self-onboard, `release_manifest.mjs --write`. Author scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs on the runBee/record conventions at scripts/test_conformance.mjs:44,95-97,51-55, with a negative-control row proving compact-check mutated nothing.

truths: Both verbs appear in `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs --help --json` with parameters, description and runnable examples derived from the registry entry.
test_bee_cli.mjs is green: both new entries had their examples executed, and the DA5 bijection holds in both directions.
state compact-check exits non-zero only on a usage error, never on a detected mismatch.
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check are green at cap time.
artifacts: Two registry entries, two handlers, two dispatch-map lines, the Use-line update, new test_bee_cli rows, refreshed mirrors and manifest, scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs
prohibitions: Never register state compact-capsule here — its builder does not exist until cz-5 and the coverage gate is unconditional.
Never edit .bee/bin/bee.mjs as the source.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs && node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

title: The compact capsule, its golden, and the verb that renders it
action: Per D6 (as superseded by D19)/D7/D8/D15/D24/D26. STEP 1 — extract three renderers from buildSessionPreamble into exported helpers, output BYTE-IDENTICAL: the onboarding-MISSING line (:318-326), the gate-bypass banner (:342-354), and the HANDOFF block (:369-387). THE HANDOFF RENDERER TAKES BOTH `handoff` AND `handoffOutcome` (D26): handoffOutcome is NOT always null on compact — hooks/bee-session-init.mjs:113-121 sets {ok:false, code:'WRONG_SOURCE'} whenever a planned-next handoff exists on a non-adopting source, and inject.mjs:384-386 renders an `- Adoption not applied: …` line off it. Dropping that parameter silently loses the line and breaks byte-identity on resume in the one case no existing row covers. Decide deliberately whether the leading lines.push('') at :375 belongs to the helper or the caller. STEP 2 — add buildCompactCapsule(root,{sessionId}) to compaction.mjs emitting D6 items 2-12 IN ORDER: STATE MISMATCH when compactCheck fails; onboarding-MISSING; the HANDOFF block; the bypass banner; phase/mode/feature/lane PRESERVING the `- Phase:` label verbatim; the claimed cell with its verify and dependency status; the first open gate; next_action; the recorded commands; the survival count and D9 warning; the critical-patterns pointer. THE CAPSULE NEVER RENDERS THE ANCHOR (D19) — the hook keeps prefixing it. THE STATE MISMATCH LINE MUST NEVER BE ANCHOR-SENSITIVE: compactCheck reports 'an anchor exists' as a check, but the capsule must not vary its mismatch line on anchor presence, or two capsules that differ only by anchor will differ in body and break the additivity row at hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:2740-2780. STEP 3 — register `state compact-capsule --session-id <id>` now that its builder exists: registry entry, handler, dispatch-map line, the bee.mjs:4830 Use-list, and its own assertExampleOk call in test_bee_cli.mjs. STEP 4 — author scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs: item ORDER asserted positionally; the D15 bare-repo case; a lane-bound session showing its own lane; the STATE MISMATCH path; and byte-identity of buildSessionPreamble against a COMMITTED GOLDEN at scripts/fixtures/preamble-golden.txt with the `## bee v${BEE_VERSION}` line (inject.mjs:317) normalized before comparison. Do NOT reconstruct the golden with `git show HEAD:` — that form passes at cap and silently becomes a tautology for every later cell, since HEAD then holds the changed file. STEP 5 — D24 regen chain: mirror, render plugin trees, self-onboard, release_manifest --write.

truths: buildSessionPreamble's output matches the committed golden for startup, clear and resume, with only the version line normalized.
The golden comparison still bites after cz-5 commits — it is a committed fixture, not a git-show reconstruction.
The capsule's item order is asserted positionally, so reordering fails the suite.
The capsule renders on a bare repo emitting no warning and no mismatch line (D15).
The `- Phase:` label is unchanged and hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs is green with ZERO edits.
The extracted HANDOFF renderer still emits the adoption-refused line when handoffOutcome is WRONG_SOURCE.
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check are green at cap time.
artifacts: Three renderers, buildCompactCapsule, the compact-capsule verb, scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs, the committed golden, refreshed mirrors and manifest
prohibitions: Never render the anchor inside the capsule (D19).
Never make the STATE MISMATCH line depend on anchor presence.
Never rename or reformat the `- Phase:` label.
Never edit hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs in this cell — if it reds, the capsule is wrong.
Never build the byte-identity baseline from `git show HEAD:`.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

title: SessionStart takes the compact branch
action: Per D5/D6/D8/D19/D24. In hooks/bee-session-init.mjs: when payload.source === 'compact', append the resume compaction record and emit buildCompactCapsule instead of buildSessionPreamble; every other source keeps buildSessionPreamble byte-identical (D8). LEAVE intentLeadBlock AND ANCHOR_LEAD_SOURCES UNTOUCHED (D19) — the hook keeps prefixing the anchor on compact and resume exactly as today, so the shape stays `anchor + \n\n + body` and only the body changes. ADOPT_SOURCES and the adoption path are untouched. The hook stays a thin caller — no logic here (D3). Extend scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs with through-the-hook rows: a compact SessionStart emits anchor-then-capsule with the anchor appearing exactly ONCE, asserted by COUNT not by startsWith; a resume SessionStart still emits anchor-then-full-preamble. Then the D24 regen chain: mirror the hook, render plugin trees, self-onboard, release_manifest --write — editing hooks/ moves release_manifest --check (release_manifest.mjs:133) even though it does NOT move ledger_parity (which covers only .bee/bin/lib and helpers, ledger_parity.mjs:139-140). RED-FAILURE EVIDENCE IS REQUIRED AT CAP: write the new rows first, record their failing output against the un-wired hook, then wire it.

truths: The anchor appears exactly ONCE in a compact SessionStart's output, asserted by count.
hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs is green with zero edits, including the resume additivity row at :2740-2780 and the HANDOFF rows at :2427-2455 and :2854-2883.
The recorded red output of the new rows against the un-wired hook is attached as cap evidence.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: The compact branch, its mirror, through-the-hook test rows, refreshed manifest
prohibitions: Never modify ANCHOR_LEAD_SOURCES or intentLeadBlock.
Never edit hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs in this cell — the SessionStart rows must stay green untouched.
Never put capsule logic in the hook.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

title: The two advisory surfaces, and the PreCompact additivity row they change
action: Per D4/D5/D9/D10/D11/D23/D24. bee-session-close.mjs on PreCompact: append the precompact record, render the D9 survival warning when cell_compact_count >= 2, and emit the anchor nudge with force:true bypassing dedup (matching maybeCaptureQueueNudge's force at :412-414). All three go through emitHookOutput as a systemMessage and NEVER encodeBlock — the B2/R14 contract at :234-238. bee-prompt-context.mjs: the deduped anchor nudge via shouldInject/markInjected, key 'anchor-missing-nudge', hash <sessionId>:<feature>:<cell>. THIS CELL MUST SPLIT AN EXISTING ASSERTION (D23), and it is the reason this cell owns hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: the PreCompact additivity row at :2692-2712 asserts the with-anchor message equals the anchor block plus the no-anchor control's EXACT bytes. The D10 nudge fires precisely when readIntent is null — true in the control fixture (preControl, :2656) and false in preRoot (:2657) — and buildFixture (:240-259) writes phase 'swarming' with approved_gates.execution true, so D10's predicate is satisfied in the control. The control therefore gains a nudge line the with-anchor message lacks, and the row goes red BY DESIGN. Split it the way D19 made unnecessary for SessionStart: keep an additivity assertion over the portion both messages share, and add an explicit assertion that the no-anchor control carries the nudge while the with-anchor message does not. Do not weaken the row to a substring match. Then the D24 regen chain: mirror both hooks, render plugin trees, self-onboard, release_manifest --write. Author scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs: PreCompact output contains no decision:'block' under any input; the warning appears on a cell's second compaction and not its first; the nudge fires on PreCompact even against a freshly-marked dedup cache; the nudge is suppressed on a second UserPromptSubmit within the interval; no nudge when an anchor exists or the phase is terminal. RED-FAILURE EVIDENCE IS REQUIRED AT CAP: record the suite failing against the un-wired hooks before wiring them.

truths: PreCompact output carries no turn-control verdict of any kind, asserted against the emitted payload.
The survival warning fires on a cell's second compaction and not its first.
The PreCompact nudge survives a freshly-marked dedup cache; the UserPromptSubmit nudge does not.

The split PreCompact row still asserts additivity over the shared portion and additionally asserts the nudge's presence in the control and absence in the with-anchor message — it is not weakened to a substring match.
The recorded red output against the un-wired hooks is attached as cap evidence.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: Both wired hooks, their mirrors, the split PreCompact row, scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs, refreshed manifest
prohibitions: Never emit encodeBlock from PreCompact.
Never let the 30-minute dedup swallow the PreCompact nudge.
Never weaken the PreCompact additivity row to a substring or regex match to make it pass.
Never touch the SessionStart rows at :2740-2780, :2427-2455 or :2854-2883.

verify: node scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

title: Close the chain and prove the whole tree green
action: Per D18/D24 and PAT37. Earlier cells each owned their own regen, so this cell CONFIRMS rather than repairs. Run: (1) `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`; (2) `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`; (3) `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`; (4) confirm .bee/bin/bee.mjs matches skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs byte for byte — scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs does NOT check that pair (scripts/test_native_probe.mjs:355-357). Then the FULL verify, output recorded verbatim. DIFF CRITERION, scoped: .bee/onboarding.json's `updated_at` is rewritten unconditionally on every --apply (onboard_bee.mjs:3326), so it ALWAYS differs and is not a finding. A diff in any OTHER file from steps 1-3 means an earlier cell left its own regen undone — report it as a finding with the file names. Measured baseline for comparison: 84 suites, exit 0, ~63.6s wall at concurrency 5; the feature adds five suites, so expect ~89.

truths: `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` exits 0 with its output recorded verbatim.
A fresh onboarding dry-run reports no drift.
Whether steps 1-3 produced a diff in any file other than .bee/onboarding.json's updated_at is reported explicitly, with file names if so.
artifacts: Confirmed plugin trees, manifest and ledger, plus the recorded full-verify output
prohibitions: Never hand-edit .bee/onboarding.json hashes.
Do not cap on a partial verify.
Do not report the updated_at churn as a finding, and do not let it mask a real one.
